rag_rules: Place assume/guarantee declarations in component type scopes when they describe interface behavior, and in implementation scopes for subcomponent bindings.
Use `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks to contain AGREE contract elements.
generic_rules: Use visible identifiers and feature names in AGREE contracts to ensure scope validity.
Structure AGREE equations and constants within component type annexes for interface-level contracts.
Place implementation-specific assignments in component implementation annexes.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE syntax uses `assume "label": expression;` and `guarantee "label": expression;` for contracts.
Equations and constants are declared with `eq name : type = expression;` and `const name : type = value;`.
anti_patterns: Avoid placing implementation-level bindings in component type annexes unless they refer to valid visible outputs.
Do not mix component type and implementation scope elements in the same annex block without proper scoping.
